summary: First-pass testing of ServeSync (Expo + FastAPI + MongoDB). Backend: 17/17 pytest cases pass covering rooms CRUD, menu categories, items (incl. availability toggle & price update), orders create/edit/status transitions, stats, WebSocket order_created broadcast, and regenerate-code cascade. Frontend: Verified role picker, tablet setup landing with pairing code chip, 4-column Orders board, Menu tab with horizontal category chips (single row, no wrap), Settings sheet open, waiter pair (accepts 4-digit numeric + name), waiter menu with alphanumeric table selector (accepted 'T7' & 'Z9'), cart, submit-to-kitchen, and real-time WebSocket propagation to tablet in <5s. Finish-order (green) button on Ready column card verified visible & clickable, transitions status to served. Waiter received in-app toast/notification after status change. No red-screen errors observed.
action_items: Optional polish: waiter `router.push('/waiter/(tabs)/orders')` uses a grouped-route path; consider `router.push('/waiter/orders')` for consistency (not blocking, current behaviour still navigates correctly).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py exclude of `_id` handled via clean() helper on all reads/broadcasts – good.
WebSocket path correctly prefixed under /api and validates room existence with 4404 close.

Frontend API layer uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (Expo web variant) – matches .env; not the EXPO_BACKEND_URL name mentioned in generic guidance, which is fine because Expo web only exposes EXPO_PUBLIC_* to the client bundle.
seed_data_creation: Created transient test rooms via POST /api/rooms with names prefixed 'TEST_' / 'UI_TEST' / 'FIN_TEST'; each seeded default menu (Starters, Mains, Drinks, Desserts).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend base URL: EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. Test file: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (17 tests, ~3s runtime). Frontend cross-context flow: seed localStorage keys 'servesync.role', 'servesync.code' (and 'servesync.waiter_name' for waiter) as JSON strings (quoted) then navigate to /tablet or /waiter directly. WebSocket at /api/ws/{code} delivers 'connected', 'order_created', 'order_updated' events.
